name: directory-list-2.3-medium.txt
purpose: Classic DirBuster list of directories and paths, sorted by how often they appear on real-world websites. The baseline for brute-forcing site structure.
whenToUse: The primary list for the first full directory pass on most targets.

name: directory-list-2.3-big.txt
purpose: Extended version of the DirBuster medium list with a larger number of rare directories and paths. Deep enumeration of site structure.
whenToUse: When medium produced no results and you need a deeper but longer-running pass.

name: directory-list-lowercase-2.3-medium.txt
purpose: Version of the DirBuster medium list converted to lowercase. Fewer duplicates when fuzzing case-insensitive servers.
whenToUse: Used instead of the regular medium on case-insensitive servers (IIS, many configs) to avoid running duplicates.

name: raft-large-directories.txt
purpose: List of directories from the RAFT project, collected from real applications. A quality alternative to DirBuster for directory enumeration.
whenToUse: When you need a directory-focused list more modern than DirBuster; often used as a second pass.

name: raft-large-files.txt
purpose: List of file names from the RAFT project. Aimed at finding specific files: .env, .bak, configs, backups, logs.
whenToUse: When you are looking specifically for files (leaked configs and backups) rather than directories; usually together with web-extensions.txt.

name: raft-large-words.txt
purpose: List of "words" from RAFT with no tie to a type (directory/file). Universal path tokens for flexible fuzzing with any extension.
whenToUse: When you fuzz path segments and supply the extensions yourself via -e; a universal pass.

name: common.txt
purpose: Short list of the most common directories and files (the dirb baseline). A fast initial scan without noise.
whenToUse: The very first, fast pass on a new target to immediately catch the obvious.

name: big.txt
purpose: Extended list of common paths (the dirb baseline). Broader coverage than common.txt while staying a reasonable size.
whenToUse: A second pass after common.txt, when the fast scan turned up nothing but the heavy DirBuster lists are still too early.

name: quickhits.txt
purpose: List of "quick hits": sensitive files and paths (.git, .env, backups, admin panels, configs) that often yield a finding right away.
whenToUse: Right at the start as a fast low-hanging-fruit pass in parallel with the main scan.

name: web-extensions.txt
purpose: List of file extensions (.php, .bak, .zip, .old, etc.). Not a standalone wordlist, but a set of extensions for substitution.
whenToUse: Attached via the -e/-x flag together with a word list to enumerate files with different extensions.

name: onelistforall.txt
purpose: Aggregate wordlist by six2dez: a deduplicated union of the best short lists from SecLists, assetnote and others. One list "for everything" for content discovery.
whenToUse: When you do not want to juggle wordlists and need one balanced list for a full pass.

name: onelistforallmicro.txt
purpose: Trimmed, hand-curated version of OneListForAll: only the most valuable paths with no bloat.
whenToUse: For a fast but meaningful pass, when the full onelistforall.txt is too heavy.

name: dicc.txt (dirsearch)
purpose: The default dirsearch wordlist: a compact, cleaned-up list of paths and files with support for the %EXT% placeholder for extensions.
whenToUse: Used automatically when dirsearch is run without -w; a good balanced default.

name: httparchive_directories_1m (assetnote)
purpose: Auto-generated assetnote list of directories from the top-1M HTTP Archive sites, sorted by frequency. Reflects the real paths of the modern web.
whenToUse: When classic DirBuster/raft are outdated and you need a fresh directory wordlist based on real statistics.

name: api/api-endpoints.txt (SecLists)
purpose: List of typical API endpoint segments from SecLists (users, login, token, v1, account, etc.) for enumerating API paths.
whenToUse: First pass over a REST API: enumerating standard endpoints under /api/ or /v1/.

name: api/objects.txt (SecLists)
purpose: List of API object/resource names (entities like user, order, product) for enumerating REST collections and resource paths.
whenToUse: When the /api/<object> structure is known and you need to enumerate the resources/collections themselves.

name: httparchive_apiroutes (assetnote)
purpose: Auto-generated assetnote list of real API routes from HTTP Archive. The best wordlist for contextual discovery of API endpoints on the modern web.
whenToUse: The primary list for serious API path enumeration, when the SecLists api-* lists are not enough.

name: swagger-wordlist.txt (assetnote)
purpose: Assetnote wordlist assembled from public Swagger/OpenAPI specifications. Real API method and path names found in documentation.
whenToUse: For deep API enumeration, especially when a REST API following typical Swagger schemas is suspected.
